structural_claim: Collective phenomena emerge from microscopic distribution functions through resonant interactions that fundamentally alter system dynamics beyond single-particle descriptions

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Wave damping emerges from velocity distribution function structure through resonant particle-wave interactions without collisions
Plasma dynamics is fundamentally described by phase space distribution evolution
Macroscopic quantum state emerges from microscopic distribution when particles occupy the same quantum state
Fermionic distribution constraints fundamentally determine allowed collective states
